title: caucalis neglecta
intro: Caucalis neglecta, also known as Wild Carrot or Wild Queen Anne's Lace, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and promoting respiratory health. Caucalis neglecta can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in folk remedies for its antiseptic and expectorant properties.
